response.justification: Bogdan holds a current Staff Engineer title with hands-on AI-agent and MCP tooling work that maps directly to the AI-native environment described. His Sociomantic stint demonstrates genuine high-throughput distributed systems experience at meaningful scale. The blocking concern is Python: it is listed as a must-have with 'strong' proficiency expected, yet it does not appear anywhere in his skills, projects, or employment history. His distributed systems depth is also several years stale, with current work skewing toward TypeScript product and developer tooling. Worth a screen call specifically to probe Python exposure and whether his architectural currency extends to modern distributed infrastructure.
strengths: Current AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq — MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — directly satisfies the AI-native environment requirement
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O architecture
Strong ownership track record: founded and sustains an open-source geo-data platform, took over iOS development mid-project, and has held Staff titles across multiple employers
concerns: Python is entirely absent from skills, projects, and all role descriptions despite being listed as a must-have with 'strong' proficiency in the JD
High-throughput distributed systems work is concentrated in a single 2018–2020 role; current and recent positions are product/tooling-focused with no evidence of infrastructure-at-scale ownership

No mention of on-call rotations, incident response, or cross-functional stakeholder coordination — operational readiness and staff-level influence across product and leadership are explicit requirements
